LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BTIG, LLC'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL AND DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING CLAIMS, AND THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE ARBITRATION OF THE UCL CLAIM. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS). ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Seth Rafkin, Esq. and Jennifer Bogue, Esq. 979-891-43370 of Rafkin for Plaintiff.
Nancy Pritikin, Esq. 650-815-2691 and Ryan Bust, Esq. 415-774-3116 of Sheppard Mullin for Defendant BTIG.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BTIG, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration is granted as to the fourth cause of action for violation of the UCL and denied as to the remaining claims, and the action is stayed pending the arbitration of the UCL claim.

(See word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
